meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Work Order for extension of NeSDA Way Forward PMU from 01.04.2024 to 31.3.2025
meta.deliverables: Generation of NeSDA Way Forward monthly reports focusing on unified e-service delivery portals, conversion of offline services to e-services, faceless mandatory e-services, and suo-moto e-service delivery. Monitoring and updating NeSDA Way Forward Dashboard, analyzing uploaded inputs, identifying implementation bottlenecks, and disseminating best practices. Conducting meetings and brainstorming sessions with State Governments, RTS Chief Commissioners/Appellate Officers to improve e-service delivery. Facilitating DARPG to conduct National e-Governance Webinars (NeGW). Providing handholding and query resolution for SPoCs, RTS Chief Commissioners/Appellate Officers, NIC, session speakers, and other key stakeholders. Any other work assigned by DARPG.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances (DARPG) under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions extends the Quality Council of India (QCI) Project Management Unit (PMU) for NeSDA Way Forward from 01.04.2024 to 31.03.2025. The PMU team of 3 QCI professionals will generate monthly NeSDA reports, monitor the dashboard, identify bottlenecks, conduct stakeholder meetings, facilitate national webinars, and provide handholding support. The total cost is Rs 65,52,000 (Rupees sixty-five lakhs fifty-two thousand only) plus GST, paid in five 20% milestone-based instalments. Professionals must have relevant qualifications; at least one must have Tier-1 data-analytics expertise. The SPOC from DARPG will coordinate project requirements.
